text:
There was a girl named Willow. She lived alone in an old treehouse. One day she found an old leather journal in an antique shop. She read every entry. She began keeping one herself. She wrote her dreams in crayon.

Once, she wanted to explore the forest. But her mama warned her. So she stayed home. She learned about her Mama instead.

One night an owl flew past her window. It whispered secrets. Willow listened.

She learned that Mama loved exploring. That she’d taken her canoe into the fjords and back. She’d rowed past glaciers. And somehow she’d turned those adventures into stories.

Willow wrote again. She started telling those tales.

One summer she went camping. The stars weren’t bright enough. But her tales? They shone.

Her adventures continued. She sailed a little boat. She read everything. She wrote more.

And she never talked much.

But when someone shared a story.

Willow listened.

That’s how she learned
